description_long: This object is a compact, black submachine gun with a streamlined, utilitarian design reminiscent of mid-20th-century military firearms. The body is primarily constructed from metal, with a matte finish and minimal ornamentation, emphasizing function over form. It features a straight, box-shaped magazine inserted vertically in front of the trigger guard, and a cylindrical barrel that extends from a rectangular receiver. The grip is ergonomically shaped and appears to be made of a different, possibly polymer material for comfort. The stock is integrated into the rear of the receiver, with a rounded, solid design and no visible folding or telescoping mechanism. Iron sights are present, with a simple front post and a rear notch, both fixed to the top of the receiver and barrel.
description: A compact, matte black submachine gun with a straight box magazine, cylindrical barrel, and integrated solid stock. It features simple fixed iron sights and a utilitarian, all-metal construction.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the gun, including the straight box magazine, cylindrical barrel, and solid integrated stock.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle, narrow barrel profile, and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the magazine, grip, and the overall symmetry of the receiver and stock.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the stock and the rear sight alignment.
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: simple fixed front post and rear notch
